pairs.question: What were Geoffrey Stephen Bozman's primary occupations and titles?
pairs.answer: Geoffrey Stephen Bozman was an English first-class cricketer and an officer in the Indian Civil Service. He also held the ranks of lieutenant (temporary and permanent) during his military service and was later appointed as a secretary in the Department of Education, Health and Lands in British India.

pairs.question: Describe Geoffrey Stephen Bozman's military career during World War I.
pairs.answer: Bozman was commissioned into the Queen's Royal Regiment (West Surrey) as a second lieutenant in October 1915. He transferred to the Royal Flying Corps as a flying officer in August 1916, was promoted to temporary lieutenant in March 1917, and achieved the rank in full by July 1917. He was transferred to the unemployed list in January 1919 and resigned his commission in August 1919, retaining the rank of lieutenant.

pairs.question: What significant honors were bestowed upon Geoffrey Stephen Bozman?
pairs.answer: In 1938, Bozman was made a Companion to the Order of the Indian Empire. He was later awarded a Companion of the Order of the Star of India in the 1946 New Year Honours.

pairs.question: How did Geoffrey Stephen Bozman contribute to the Indian Civil Service?
pairs.answer: Bozman joined the Indian Civil Service in 1922, working in the Department of Education, Health and Lands. He eventually rose to the rank of secretary within the department.

pairs.question: Provide details about Geoffrey Stephen Bozman's cricket career.
pairs.answer: Bozman played first-class cricket for the Europeans cricket team in the Madras Presidency Match of January 1926. In the match, he opened the batting twice and was dismissed for 20 runs in the first innings by B. Bhaskar Rao and for 4 runs in the second innings by C. R. Ganapathy.

pairs.question: What was Geoffrey Stephen Bozman's educational background?
pairs.answer: Bozman was educated at Whitgift School and later matriculated to Brasenose College, Oxford.

pairs.question: Where and when did Geoffrey Stephen Bozman pass away?
pairs.answer: Bozman retired to England and died at Bromley in February 1973.
